notes: Plaintiff opening work notes. Opportunity openings:plaintiff. Record checked with get_case/list_evidence/stat/read. Only visible evidence is ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, market-page.txt, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, 4971 bytes, case packet. It proves rule text and market metadata, not underlying event. Rule elements: Iran initiated; non-weather; by May 24, 2026 11:59 PM ET; major closure as broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting/arriving/departing Iranian airspace or major Iranian region, or complete non-weather suspension at at least two listed airports; primary resolution sources official Iranian aviation authorities and credible reporting consensus. Native investigation for opening only, not record proof: searches for May 24 2026 Iran airspace closure, Iran Civil Aviation Organization airspace closure May 2026, Flightradar24, western airspace Iran May 24 2026, Civil Aviation Authority western Iran May 24. Opened Gulf News May 23 article, Gulf News May 24 page, Newswire May 23, and Polymarket event page as leads. Lead trail suggests a May 23 western Tehran FIR/airspace restriction or NOTAM, possible suspension of civil flight permits in western sector, and limited daytime operations at eight sites. Adverse/uncertain points: may be regional western FIR only, not full national closure; eight airports may have remained operational; need exact NOTAM/CAO text and airport-by-airport effect; need whether IKA/THR/MHD/SYZ/IFN affected and whether arrivals/departures/transits were suspended; need credible reporting consensus and official source capture in later evidence phase. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md. Opening theory: Plaintiff should frame the rule and burden; later evidence can show a security-driven official western Iranian airspace closure satisfied major-region language despite exceptions, unless adverse proof shows mere warnings, foreign/airline restrictions, minor delays, VFR/private limits, weather, or insufficient commercial suspension.

Opening defendant work notes. Record inspected with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Visible evidence: only ev_e61df84283cc_market-page, sha256 e61df84283cc858bfd0cf8235c6f1d53a10d6e1c37b2d97256da8f1e6391cc17, 4971-byte text case packet. Plaintiff opening exists; no arguments, exhibits, submitted evidence, technical reports, rebuttals, surrebuttals, or closings.

Packet analysis: The packet proves the governing market rule and burden. It does not prove any May 2026 Iranian closure occurred. Rule requires Iran-initiated, non-weather closure by May 24 2026 11:59 PM ET. Major closure means broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major Iranian airspace region; limited cancellations/delays/partial closures do not qualify, though limited exceptions to a broad closure do not disqualify. Alternative path: non-weather complete suspension of commercial arrivals and departures at at least two listed airports (IKA, THR, MHD, SYZ, IFN). Primary sources: official Iranian aviation authority information and consensus credible reporting.

Source map: plaintiff likely needs official Iranian CAA/CAO NOTAM or statement plus credible reporting. Defendant focus: actual wording and scope, whether it was only western Tehran FIR/airport-permit restrictions, whether eight airports stayed operational, whether commercial flights continued, whether two listed airports had complete arrivals/departures suspension, and whether evidence is primary or merely secondary.

Native investigation before opening: Web searches for Iran May 24 2026 airspace closure, Reuters Iran airspace May 24, NOTAM May 24 Tehran FIR, western airspace May 2026. Opened Gulf News and Indian Express leads; both are unadmitted secondary reports suggesting a May 22-May 25 western Tehran FIR restriction/NOTAM, suspension of civil flight permits in western sector, eight airports remaining operational with daylight/per-flight approval. Opened Safe Airspace Iran for broader context. These are source targets for later evidence, not admitted proof during opening. Search did not yet produce official NOTAM text in record.

Opening decision: Defendant should state that current record proves only the rule; plaintiff bears burden of admitting official/faithful primary material. The likely dispute is not weather but major-vs-partial scope and commercial-flight effect. A western-sector restriction with ongoing approved/daylight operations may fail unless actual admitted source shows a generally applicable broad commercial closure or complete suspension at two listed airports. Need preserve in later phase: official NOTAM OIIX A1010/26 if available, Iranian authority source, credible reports, flight/airport operation data, and adverse checks.

Plaintiff merits work notes. Opportunity arguments:plaintiff. Record rechecked after defendant opening with get_case/list/stat. Before admissions, evidence remained only ev_e61df84283cc_market-page plus openings. Submitted four source captures: ev_d98e4358b620 (Gulf News May 23; Iran CAO announced temporary closure over western Iran; all civil permits suspended in western Tehran FIR; only eight airports operational with daylight/fresh approval limits), ev_b6a489acc56c (Gulf News live section; Iran CAA issued NOTAM restricting operations at most western airports in Tehran FIR until early May 25, limited daytime flights at eight sites), ev_4e23315ca668 (Abdi Media; NOTAM A1010/26, all western Tehran FIR airports closed except limited listed airports, May 22 00:16 Iran time to May 25 noon, previous permissions canceled, new CAO authorization for IFR passenger flights), ev_0e132c49e091 (Indian Express; NOTAM OIIX A1010/26, western half/Tehran FIR shutdown May 22-May 25, eight operational airports, daylight and per-flight approval, rerouting). Record scan after admission confirmed all four are visible submitted evidence with hashes. Assessment: the strongest point is convergence on official Iranian aviation action, NOTAM identifier, timing before deadline, non-weather/security rationale, and broad western Tehran FIR closure with limited exceptions. Rule permits major Iranian airspace region and limited exceptions. Adverse points: original NOTAM/official CAO page not captured; Indian Express partly attributes to Gulf News; Abdi Media is less established; eight airports remained operational, including IKA/THR/MHD/SYZ in one report, which defense may use to argue not complete at listed airports. Plaintiff should argue under the broad major-region prong, not rely primarily on the two-listed-airport complete-suspension prong. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Defendant arguments work notes. Record/evidence scanned with list_evidence/stat_evidence/read_evidence_range. Plaintiff evidence read: ev_d98e... Gulf News May 23 says temporary western Iran closure, civil flight permits suspended at airports in western Tehran FIR, eight airports operational including Mehrabad/IKA/Isfahan/Yazd, daylight and fresh approval. ev_b6... Gulf News live says NOTAM restricting operations at most western airports, limited daytime flights at eight sites. ev_4e... Abdi Media gives A1010/26 and states all airports in western Tehran FIR closed except OITR/OIKK/OIAA/OISS/OIYY/OICC/OIGG/OIAW sunrise-sunset, previous permissions canceled, new authorization required for civil IFR passenger flights, valid May 22-May 25. ev_0e... Indian Express says OIIX A1010/26, western half/western sector closure, eight airports operational including several listed market airports, daylight/per-flight approval, rerouting; partly relies on Gulf News.

Native investigation: searched exact NOTAM identifiers and raw phrases: "A1010/26" "OIIX", "OIIX A1010/26" NOTAM, "A1010/26" "Tehran FIR", "May 22" "A1010/26" Iran NOTAM, site:caa.gov.ir A1010/26 OIIX, site:cao.ir A1010/26 NOTAM Iran, "OIIX/QFALC", "ALL AIRPORTS IN WEST PART OF TEHRAN FIR ARE CLSD". Found Notamify page with raw NOTAM; no official Iranian archive found in quick searches and no official denial found. Submitted Notamify page as defendant evidence ev_e727676aa10f_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-e727676aa10f.

Defense evidence significance: Notamify captures raw A1010/26: Q)OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999; A)OIIX B)2605222046 C)2605250830EST; E) all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except listed airports operational sunrise to sunset; previous permissions suspended; new permission required from CAA for operation of civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. Notamify categorizes it as AD Airport Operations. This confirms official-ish NOTAM text but supports the defense distinction: airport operations restriction, not express enroute airspace/transit closure. It also shows continued civil IFR passenger operations possible at excepted airports.

Merits theory: Plaintiff has evidence of a security-related airport restriction by Iran before the deadline, but the rule requires a major closure of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from Iranian airspace or a major airspace region, or complete suspension at two listed airports. Plaintiff does not prove overflight/transit closure from raw NOTAM, and their own evidence shows exceptions/continued operations. The separate listed-airport prong is not proven and plaintiff effectively abandoned it; sources conflict on which airports were operational. Provenance: no direct Iranian official archive; plaintiff uses secondary captures, defendant uses third-party raw NOTAM display. Under preponderance, ambiguity about scope should weigh against plaintiff.

Plaintiff rebuttal notes. Record scanned with list/stat/read. New defendant evidence: ev_e727676aa10f_submitted-evidence-05-defendant-e727676aa10f, Notamify raw NOTAM A1010/26. Exact read shows Q-line OIIX/QFALC/IV/NBO/A/000/999, applicability UTC 2026-05-22 20:46 to 2026-05-25 08:30 EST, text: all airports in west part of Tehran FIR are closed except OITR, OIKK, OIAA, OISS, OIYY, OICC, OIGG, OIAW operational sunrise to sunset; all previous permissions suspended for all operators; new permission requested from CAA for operation of civil IFR passenger flights on these airports. Defense theory: QFALC/AD Airport Operations means not airspace/transit closure, only partial airport restriction. Rebuttal theory: defense evidence corroborates the key closure; the market rule covers broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights arriving/departing from a major Iranian airspace region, with limited exceptions permitted. Airport-operations classification does not negate that all airports in west part of Tehran FIR were closed except narrow approved airports, previous permissions were suspended for all operators, and civil IFR passenger flights required new CAA permission. That is a broad regional commercial-flight suspension with limited exceptions, not normal continuing operations. No new evidence needed; rely on PX-1 to PX-5 and DX-1. Journal updated.

Surrebuttal notes. Record checked with get_case/list_evidence/stat_evidence. Plaintiff filed rebuttal; no new submitted evidence other than offering defendant Notamify raw NOTAM as PX-R1. Rebuttal argues DX-1 corroborates a broad regional closure, that QFALC/AD Airport Operations is only a notice category not a market-rule limit, that limited CAA-approved exceptions are expressly allowed by PX-1, and that transit need not be separately shown because the closure of all western FIR airports plus suspended permissions and rerouting suffice.

Targeted investigation: Searched for NOTAM Q-code meaning: "ICAO NOTAM QFALC aerodrome closed scope A Q code" and "NOTAM QFALC aerodrome closed Q code scope A". Opened FAA Order 7930.2 Appendix B International NOTAM Q Codes. It states NOTAM Q-code group has five letters; second/third identify subject; fourth/fifth identify operational status. Relevant entries: FA = Aerodrome; AF = Flight information region; AR = ATS route; LC = Closed. Submitted this as defendant evidence ev_f5c3c89cf1da_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-f5c3c89cf1da.

Surrebuttal theory: Concede serious Iran-issued security restriction, but plaintiff still must cross the threshold of major closure under PX-1. Limited-exceptions language applies only after a broad closure is proven; it does not transform an aerodrome-closure NOTAM into an FIR/route/transit airspace closure. Raw A1010/26 plus FAA code table shows QFALC means aerodrome closed; the body closes airports in west Tehran FIR, except listed airports, and permits civil IFR passenger flights with new CAA permission. PX-5 rerouting is circumstantial avoidance and cannot substitute for proof of legal prohibition on commercial transit. Listed-airport prong remains unproven and effectively abandoned.

Plaintiff closing notes. Closing opportunity closings:plaintiff. Record scanned with list/stat/read. New defendant surrebuttal evidence is ev_f5c3c89cf1da_submitted-evidence-06-defendant-f5c3c89cf1da, FAA Q-code table. Exact read: QFA means Aerodrome; LC means Closed; LI closed to IFR; LV closed to VFR; separate airspace/FIR/ATS route subject codes exist. Defense uses this to argue A1010/26 is airport-only, not airspace. Closing assessment: concede QFALC means aerodrome closed, but argue market rule is not keyed to Q-code taxonomy. It covers broad closure/cancellation/suspension of commercial flights in a major Iranian airspace region and expressly permits limited exceptions. A regionwide closure of all airports in west part of Tehran FIR, suspension of all prior permissions, and new CAA permission for civil IFR passenger flights at only exception airports is a broad regional commercial flight suspension; PX-5 adds rerouting for transit context. Weakness remains original Iranian official NOTAM archive not in record and transit prong not as clean as arrivals/departures, but preponderance favors plaintiff because defense evidence corroborates the core closure and no weather/foreign-only/ordinary-operation contrary evidence exists. Journal updated at /aar-codex/aar-work-notes.md.

Closing notes. Record/evidence scanned with list_evidence; stat checked key defense evidence ev_e727676aa10f Notamify raw NOTAM and ev_f5c3c89cf1da FAA Q-code extract. Plaintiff closing filed; no new evidence in closing. Plaintiff strongest closing points: rule does not require national shutdown or particular NOTAM taxonomy; core facts show Iran authority NOTAM before deadline for western Tehran FIR, non-weather/security context, suspended civil permits, daylight exceptions, new CAA approval, and rerouting; defense raw NOTAM confirms all airports in west part closed, all previous permissions suspended, and new CAA permission required; limited exceptions are allowed by rule.

Defense closing plan: Concede serious Iran-issued security restriction. Emphasize burden and exact rule: broad closure/suspension of commercial flights transiting, arriving in, and departing from airspace or major region, or complete suspension at two listed airports. The best evidence remains raw NOTAM + FAA table: QFALC decodes as aerodrome closed; body closes airports in west Tehran FIR with named exceptions and permits civil IFR passenger flights with new CAA permission. It does not close FIR, ATS routes, or overflights. Rerouting from PX-5 is secondary/circumstantial and could reflect avoidance. Listed-airport prong is abandoned and unproven. Missing official archive, route chart, flight dataset, and airport movement records leave scope ambiguity plaintiff has to bear under preponderance.
